refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into two key sub-functions: acquiring timely data from the worksite and processing that data efficiently to update the model.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure and verify the latency of data acquisition and processing separately and combined, ensuring the p95 latency is ≤ 1.0 s under supported update scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-frequency telemetry data streams with priority network scheduling
DP2: Implement edge computing for local data pre-processing
DP3: Optimize data processing algorithms for low-latency model updates
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP3 combined: Use high-frequency telemetry data streams with priority network scheduling and optimize data processing algorithms for low-latency model updates

potential_sources: Tight coupling between telemetry data acquisition and processing algorithms
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized to minimize cross-dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link supports priority scheduling and sufficient bandwidth
Risk of processing algorithm complexity causing latency spikes
Assumes telemetry data quality is consistent
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding edge computing (DP2) if network constraints degrade performance.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry data acquisition, processing, and worksite model update

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives high-frequency telemetry data streams from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports priority scheduling for telemetry data transmission and control messages

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes telemetry data acquisition and processing, minimizing diffusion.
Vehicle provides telemetry data but does not process updates.
Teleoperation consumes telemetry but does not own update processing.
Safety Services unrelated to update latency function.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the update latency function within the NGHE Solution module reduces interface complexity and diffusion risk.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes too complex
Dependency on network link performance outside NGHE Solution control
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor complexity growth in NGHE Solution and consider modularizing processing algorithms if needed.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are logically decomposed and mapped to candidate DPs that address latency through data acquisition and processing optimization.

diffusion_risks: Potential diffusion if telemetry acquisition and processing are split across multiple modules
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Assigning both acquisition and processing to NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and interface complexity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring latency end-to-end using telemetry and network link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of update latency function within NGHE Solution module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Consider contingency plans if network link performance degrades
Evaluate adding edge computing module if latency targets are not met

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and mapping to DPs and IMs meets the latency requirement with clear ownership and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network link supports priority scheduling and sufficient bandwidth
open_risks: Processing algorithm complexity causing latency spikes
